text: Literally, ‘Which thou didst make ready beforehand from the beginning’. The rendering given assumes a reference to the tabernacle in the desert, and the pattern of it shewn to Moses on Mount Sinai. Others would interpret the verse as alluding to God’s dwelling-place in heaven.
Or perhaps, ‘Wisdom is ever at thy side’.
This verse is represented in the Greek text by four words, meaning ‘And were saved (or, healed) by wisdom’.
